title: rubus candicans var. coarctatus
intro: Rubus candicans var. coarctatus is a lesser-known variety of the black raspberry plant, a type of bramble native to the tropical regions of Asia, characterized by its dark green leaves and vibrant pink flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its high content of antioxidants, anthocyanins, and ellagic acid, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory, anti-cancer, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rubus candicans var. coarctatus include its potential to prevent cardiovascular disease, reduce the risk of certain cancers, and support immune function. In herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, or used to make jams, jellies, and other value-added products that can be consumed for their health-promoting properties.
